goal: Continuously improve the local Codex agent without paid APIs by combining deterministic local execution, browser DOM control, KVM fallback, Git checkpoints, and structured consultation with Gemini and ChatGPT web UIs.

codexSolo: Deterministic local file edits, shell commands, git work, backup logic, and test execution.
Stable DOM actions on pages with known selectors and predictable flows.
Benchmarking, logging, retry logic, health checks, and other runtime plumbing.
consultGemini: Current web UI structure, selector drift, screenshot interpretation, and Google product behavior.
Latest-service behavior where a live page interpretation matters more than pure code reasoning.
Large text summarization when the source lives in the browser and fast screen or context reading helps.
consultChatGPT: Script design, refactoring options, structured operating rules, and change summaries.
Tradeoff analysis between multiple implementation paths.
Backlog shaping, architecture wording, and report-ready explanations.
consultBoth: High-risk design changes that affect the runtime architecture.
New operating policies that will govern future autonomous behavior.
Cases where Gemini provides page or UI context and ChatGPT provides code or structure context.
fallbacks: If a provider tab is stale, choose another responsive client for the same provider before giving up.
If DOM control fails, inspect the screen and only then use KVM fallback.
If one provider is unavailable, continue with the other provider plus official docs and record the gap.

borrowedPatterns.name: Single gateway as source of truth
borrowedPatterns.applyToLocalAgent: Keep one orchestrator responsible for session state, routing, and channel health instead of scattering control logic.

borrowedPatterns.name: Agent loop with explicit tool results
borrowedPatterns.applyToLocalAgent: Every action should return an observable result, and loops should stop on timeout or iteration limits.

borrowedPatterns.name: Structured action surface
borrowedPatterns.applyToLocalAgent: Prefer concise actions like navigate, prompt-send, click-text, extract, and visible-text over free-form UI driving.

borrowedPatterns.name: Local execution and repair loop
borrowedPatterns.applyToLocalAgent: Run generated scripts locally, capture stdout or stderr, and feed only the minimal failure context back into the consultation loop.

borrowedPatterns.name: Subordinate specialization with clean context
borrowedPatterns.source: Agent Zero and Codex app multi-agent guidance
borrowedPatterns.applyToLocalAgent: Keep one main orchestrator, but split work into internal workers or isolated subtasks instead of one giant mixed context.

taskCheckpoints: Write or update runtime logs for each provider interaction and each local action.
Create a git checkpoint when a meaningful improvement lands.
Store user-facing reports under 02_보고서 and task-specific notes under 01_내_요청.
Capture a screenshot when UI behavior differs from DOM assumptions.
Record what was verified, what failed, and what remains risky.
nextBacklog: Add provider health status reporting to the launcher UI.
Add response-complete detection instead of fixed waits.
Add a structured extract action for DOM pages.
Add task queueing with per-task timeouts and retries.
Add a stop file or manual pause signal for long improvement loops.
Add browser session snapshots for reproducible bug reports.
